content.text: Steward Review Cadence and Exception Handling

A governance programme that runs reviews on an ad-hoc basis is indistinguishable from one with no programme at all. The review cadence is the structured schedule that converts stewardship intent into repeatable operational discipline: monthly automated checks catch SLA breaches before they accumulate, quarterly human reviews validate that the knowledge base remains fit for purpose, and annual re-confirmations reset accountability assignments. Exception handling is the complementary process that routes decisions that cannot wait for a scheduled review — label disputes, urgent ingestion requests, or regulatory deadlines — to the right level of authority without bypassing the governance controls.

This topic covers the three-tier cadence (monthly/quarterly/annual), the data model for tracking review events, the four common exception types and their resolution paths, and the escalation model that prevents exceptions from becoming unresolved governance debt.

attrs.alt: Steward review cadence timeline and exception handling escalation

content.text: SLA breach alerts; new overdue-review items; ingestion queue length; exception register status
SLA breach report emailed to Governance Lead; exception escalations triggered automatically
Full KB label audit (sample); zero-retrieval cleanup; permitted-application list review; retraction status; DPO quarterly summary
QuarterlyReviewRecord written to governance store; retraction decisions executed; KPIs updated
Steward re-confirmation (resign or replace); classification taxonomy refresh; handling policy update; audit package delivery; RACI review
Updated StewardshipAssignment records; policy version increment; audit package signed by DPO

from __future__ import annotations
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional
from approval import IngestionApprovalRequest, ApprovalStatus
from domain_decision import DomainStewardDecision

@dataclass
class MonthlyCheckReport:
    generated_utc:     float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    period_days:       int = 30

    # SLA metrics
    pending_requests:  int = 0
    sla_breached:      int = 0
    breach_rate_pct:   float = 0.0

    # KB health
    overdue_docs:      int = 0
    approaching_docs:  int = 0    # review due in next 30 days

    # Exception register
    open_exceptions:   int = 0
    stale_exceptions:  int = 0   # open > 14 days without update

    # Actions triggered
    escalations_sent:  list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    auto_blocked_docs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # docs auto-blocked due to overdue review


def run_monthly_check(
    approval_store: list[IngestionApprovalRequest],
    decision_store: dict[str, DomainStewardDecision],
    exception_store: list[dict],
    notify_fn,
    auto_block_fn,    # callable(doc_id) -> None; prevents retrieval until reviewed
    now: Optional[float] = None,
) -> MonthlyCheckReport:
    now = now or time.time()
    report = MonthlyCheckReport()
    THIRTY_DAYS = 30 * 86400

    # --- SLA check ---
    pending = [r for r in approval_store if r.overall_status() == ApprovalStatus.PENDING]
    report.pending_requests = len(pending)
    breached = [r for r in pending if r.is_sla_breached()]
    report.sla_breached = len(breached)
    if pending:
        report.breach_rate_pct = round(100 * len(breached) / len(pending), 1)

    for req in breached:
        from escalation import check_and_escalate, StewardshipRegistry
        # In production, pass real registry; simplified here
        check_and_escalate(req, StewardshipRegistry(), notify_fn)
        report.escalations_sent.append(req.request_id)

    # --- KB document review deadlines ---
    for doc_id, decision in decision_store.items():
        if decision.is_overdue_for_review():
            report.overdue_docs += 1
            # Auto-block: prevent retrieval until steward re-confirms
            auto_block_fn(doc_id)
            report.auto_blocked_docs.append(doc_id)
        elif decision.review_by_utc - now < THIRTY_DAYS:
            report.approaching_docs += 1

    # --- Exception register staleness ---
    for exc in exception_store:
        if exc.get("status") == "open":
            report.open_exceptions += 1
            age_days = (now - exc.get("opened_utc", now)) / 86400
            if age_days > 14:
                report.stale_exceptions += 1

    return report

The quarterly review produces a structured record that serves as evidence of the stewardship programme's operation. This record is included in the annual audit package alongside the routing log coverage report and the DPO attestation letter.

from __future__ import annotations
import time, uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class ReviewOutcome(str, Enum):
    CONFIRMED    = "confirmed"     # label and permitted list unchanged
    LABEL_UPDATED = "label_updated"
    APPS_UPDATED  = "apps_updated"  # permitted_application_ids changed
    RETRACTED     = "retracted"
    EXTENDED      = "extended"      # review_by_utc pushed forward

@dataclass
class DocumentReviewEntry:
    doc_id:       str
    outcome:      ReviewOutcome
    notes:        str = ""
    new_label:    Optional[str] = None     # set if outcome == LABEL_UPDATED
    new_apps:     Optional[list[str]] = None  # set if outcome == APPS_UPDATED
    new_review_by: Optional[float] = None  # set if outcome == EXTENDED

@dataclass
class QuarterlyReviewRecord:
    review_id:       str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    kb_id:           str = ""
    quarter:         str = ""             # e.g. "2025-Q3"
    domain_steward:  str = ""
    ai_steward:      str = ""
    reviewed_utc:    float = field(default_factory=time.time)

    # Document review outcomes
    documents_reviewed: int = 0
    document_entries: list[DocumentReviewEntry] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Zero-retrieval cleanup
    zero_retrieval_docs: int = 0
    retracted_for_zero_retrieval: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Label audit sample
    label_sample_size:  int = 0
    label_corrections:  int = 0           # labels that were wrong in the sample

    # Permitted-application list changes
    apps_added:    list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    apps_removed:  list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    # DPO summary fields
    dpo_briefed:   bool = False
    dpo_notes:     str = ""

    def label_accuracy_pct(self) -> float:
        if self.label_sample_size == 0:
            return 100.0
        return round(100 * (1 - self.label_corrections / self.label_sample_size), 1)

    def summary(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"Q{self.quarter} review for KB {self.kb_id} | "
            f"Docs reviewed: {self.documents_reviewed} | "
            f"Label accuracy: {self.label_accuracy_pct()}% | "
            f"Zero-retrieval retractions: {len(self.retracted_for_zero_retrieval)} | "
            f"DPO briefed: {self.dpo_briefed}"
        )
Exceptions are decisions that cannot wait for the scheduled review cadence. Four types arise most frequently in production Gen AI governance programmes:

A requester believes the domain steward's label is too restrictive (preventing their use case) or too permissive (creating a compliance risk they have identified). The dispute resolution path preserves the principle that the domain steward has final authority within their domain, but provides a structured appeal mechanism.

from __future__ import annotations
import time, uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class DisputeResolution(str, Enum):
    PENDING         = "pending"
    STEWARD_UPHELD  = "steward_upheld"   # steward's original label stands
    REQUESTER_WIN   = "requester_win"    # label adjusted in requester's favour
    GOVERNANCE_RULING = "governance_ruling"  # Governance Lead broke the tie

@dataclass
class LabelDisputeRecord:
    dispute_id:         str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    doc_id:             str = ""
    disputed_label:     str = ""          # steward's label (being disputed)
    requester_label:    str = ""          # what the requester believes it should be
    requester_email:    str = ""
    requester_rationale: str = ""
    steward_response:   str = ""
    raised_utc:         float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    resolution:         DisputeResolution = DisputeResolution.PENDING
    resolved_label:     Optional[str] = None
    resolved_by:        Optional[str] = None
    resolved_utc:       Optional[float] = None
    governance_lead_involved: bool = False

    def resolve(self, resolution: DisputeResolution, label: str, resolver: str) -> None:
        self.resolution   = resolution
        self.resolved_label = label
        self.resolved_by  = resolver
        self.resolved_utc = time.time()
        if resolution == DisputeResolution.GOVERNANCE_RULING:
            self.governance_lead_involved = True

    def is_sla_breached(self, sla_hours: float = 48.0) -> bool:
        return (self.resolution == DisputeResolution.PENDING
                and (time.time() - self.raised_utc) / 3600 > sla_hours)

A business unit needs to use a document in an application that the domain steward has not approved. Rather than bypassing the steward, the override request goes through an expedited approval path that requires both the domain steward and the AI System Steward to sign off, with a time-limited override window that expires after 90 days and must be re-confirmed.

from __future__ import annotations
import time, uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class PermittedAppOverrideRequest:
    override_id:        str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    doc_id:             str = ""
    application_id:     str = ""
    business_justification: str = ""
    requested_by:       str = ""
    requested_utc:      float = field(default_factory=time.time)

    # Dual sign-off
    domain_approved:    Optional[bool] = None
    domain_reviewer:    Optional[str] = None
    ai_approved:        Optional[bool] = None
    ai_reviewer:        Optional[str] = None

    # Time-limited window
    override_expires_utc: Optional[float] = None  # set on approval; 90 days default
    renewal_required:   bool = True

    def is_fully_approved(self) -> bool:
        return self.domain_approved is True and self.ai_approved is True

    def is_active(self) -> bool:
        if not self.is_fully_approved():
            return False
        if self.override_expires_utc and time.time() > self.override_expires_utc:
            return False
        return True

    def approve(self, domain_ok: bool, domain_reviewer: str,
                ai_ok: bool, ai_reviewer: str, window_days: int = 90) -> None:
        self.domain_approved = domain_ok
        self.domain_reviewer = domain_reviewer
        self.ai_approved     = ai_ok
        self.ai_reviewer     = ai_reviewer
        if domain_ok and ai_ok:
            self.override_expires_utc = time.time() + window_days * 86400

A security incident, regulatory change, or product launch requires a document to be available in a Gen AI application before the normal SLA window allows. Urgent ingestion is not a way to bypass governance; it is an expedited single-steward approval with mandatory retrospective review within 5 business days.
Explicit written sign-off with business justification
URGENT metadata tag added; document visible in retrieval
Normal label verification and permitted-application decision; may override or retract
DPO acknowledges; no objection within 24h = implicit consent

When two domain stewards cannot agree on the sensitivity label or permitted-application list for a shared document, the Governance Lead acts as arbitrator. The arbitration is time-bounded: if the stewards cannot resolve within 48 hours, the Governance Lead issues a binding ruling. The ruling is documented in an `ArbitrationRecord` for audit purposes.

from __future__ import annotations
import time, uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class ArbitrationRecord:
    arbitration_id:   str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    doc_id:           str = ""
    dispute_type:     str = ""            # "label" | "permitted_apps"
    steward_a_email:  str = ""
    steward_a_position: str = ""
    steward_b_email:  str = ""
    steward_b_position: str = ""
    governance_lead:  str = ""
    raised_utc:       float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    ruling_utc:       Optional[float] = None
    ruling:           str = ""            # e.g. "Label = RESTRICTED; permitted in app X only"
    ruling_rationale: str = ""
    binding:          bool = True         # arbitration ruling overrides all steward objections

    def issue_ruling(self, ruling: str, rationale: str, ruler: str) -> None:
        self.ruling          = ruling
        self.ruling_rationale = rationale
        self.governance_lead = ruler
        self.ruling_utc      = time.time()

    def hours_to_ruling(self) -> Optional[float]:
        if self.ruling_utc is None:
            return None
        return (self.ruling_utc - self.raised_utc) / 3600

All exceptions — label disputes, override requests, urgent ingestions, and arbitration cases — are written to a central exception register. The register provides the Governance Lead with a real-time view of unresolved governance decisions and feeds the monthly SLA breach report.
Several common patterns undermine review cadence effectiveness:
100% of documents confirmed unchanged in every quarterly review
Mandate a minimum sample size for label audit; require written rationale for CONFIRMED outcomes on high-risk docs
Urgent ingestion volume exceeds 20% of total ingestion volume
Cap urgent ingestions per month; require Governance Lead approval for any team exceeding the cap
Exceptions open > 30 days with no status update
Daily automated reminders after 7 days; auto-escalate at 14 days; block new ingestions from the requester's team at 30 days
Label accuracy degrades mid-year because quarterly reviews are skipped
Enforce quarterly cadence in the governance charter; track as a KPI; tie steward re-confirmation to completion of all quarterly reviews
Governance Lead resolves disputes by choosing one steward's position without documented rationale
Require written ruling_rationale in ArbitrationRecord; publish summary to Data Governance Council

The steward review cadence creates the operational rhythm of the governance programme: monthly automated checks surface SLA breaches and overdue reviews before they accumulate; quarterly structured reviews validate label accuracy, clean zero-retrieval documents, and update permitted-application lists; annual re-confirmations reset accountability assignments and deliver the audit package. Exception handling provides the off-cadence resolution paths for label disputes (domain steward as final authority, Governance Lead as arbitrator), permitted-application overrides (dual sign-off with 90-day expiry), urgent ingestion (2-hour emergency approval, 5-day retrospective review), and cross-domain deadlocks (time-bounded arbitration). The exception register tracks all open decisions in one place and feeds the monthly SLA breach report, preventing exceptions from becoming invisible governance debt.
